items.title: AxeOS
items.description: AxeOS is the primary open-source firmware for BitAxe miners. It replaces factory firmware to provide lower latency, better pool compatibility, and advanced configuration options including DATUM support.

items.title: BitAxe
items.description: BitAxe is an open-source, low-power Bitcoin ASIC miner designed for home mining. It bridges the gap between industrial-scale equipment and hobbyist participation in network security.

items.title: Bitcoin Knots
items.description: Bitcoin Knots is an enhanced full-node implementation of Bitcoin, maintained by Luke Dashjr. It builds on Bitcoin Core with additional privacy, decentralization, and mining-related features.

items.title: Bitcoin Node Types
items.description: Comparison of full, pruned, archival, Simplified Payment Verification (SPV), and neutral nodes; explains trade-offs in storage, trust, privacy, and validation guarantees.

items.title: Braiins Hashpower
items.description: On-demand SHA-256 hashrate marketplace launched by Braiins in January 2026 (public beta). Miners buy and sell hashrate through a live order book without long-term contracts or hardware ownership.

items.title: Braiins Hashrate Autopilot
items.description: Official automation tool from Braiins for managing hashrate bids on the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Rules-based engine that buys hashrate when price crosses thresholds and auto-rebalances between pools.

items.title: C-Lightning (Core-Lightning)
items.description: Core-Lightning (CLN, often called c-lightning) is a lightweight, modular Lightning Network implementation. It is notable for early support of Bolt 12, which enables static Lightning offers for automated recurring payments.

items.title: ComparePower
items.description: Electricity plan comparison marketplace for Texas. Aggregates rates across multiple providers with zero fees to the user.

items.title: DATUM
items.description: DATUM (Decentralized Template Auction Machine / UTXO Manager) is a protocol that lets miners build custom block templates directly on their own node and submit them to a mining pool—without the pool dictating what transactions go into the block.

items.title: HashGuard
items.description: DIY Python auto-optimizer for the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Monitors the order book, detects stalled bids, and automatically adjusts pricing to maintain hashrate flow while minimizing overpayment.

items.title: HashRate Index
items.description: Data and analytics platform for Bitcoin mining. Tracks miner revenue, profitability, power consumption by rig, and historical ASIC pricing.

items.title: Hodl Up
items.description: Bitcoin mining-themed board game. Teaches proof-of-work concepts, mining economics, and hodling strategy through physical gameplay.

items.title: Home Mining
items.description: Practical guide to solo and pooled mining at home using consumer hardware, hash marketplaces, and Stratum V2 / DATUM template control.

items.title: How Do I Start Mining at Home?
items.description: Step-by-step guide to solo and pooled home mining: hardware selection, Stratum V2/DATUM template control, hash marketplace on-ramps, and operational security.

items.title: KaboomRacks
items.description: ASIC mining hardware marketplace. Sells refurbished and used miners, often at prices accessible to home miners.

items.title: Mining Decentralization
items.description: Analysis of mining centralization pressures and decentralization mechanisms: Stratum V2, DATUM, hash marketplaces, small-pool coordination, and the path toward sovereign hash-rate ownership.

items.title: Mining Like The Big Players
items.description: Date: May 14, 2026  )

items.title: Mining Payout Schemas
items.description: Comparison of PPLNS, FPPS, PPS+, TIDES, and solo mining payout models with variance analysis, latency, pool fees, and suitability for different hash-rate scales.

items.title: Mining Payout Schemas
items.description: Comparison of PPLNS, FPPS, PPS+, TIDES, and solo mining payout models with variance, latency, and pool fee implications.

items.title: mining-hashpower-datum-outline
items.description: Note: This presentation also has interactive slide-by-slide pages with key takeaways and SpeakerDeck links where available.

items.title: No-KYC Acquisition Methods
items.description: Methods for acquiring Bitcoin without identity verification including Bisq, RoboSats, HodlHodl, Azteco vouchers, Bitcoin ATMs, and in-person trades.

items.title: Ocean.xyz
items.description: Ocean.xyz is a Bitcoin mining pool that emphasizes transparency, non-custodial payouts, and miner sovereignty through the DATUM protocol.

items.title: Proof of Work & Bitcoin Supply
items.description: Fundamentals of Nakamoto consensus, difficulty adjustment, hash rate measurement, energy economics, and the security model underlying Bitcoin's immutability.

items.title: public-pool.io
items.description: public-pool.io is a zero-fee, open-source solo mining pool. Instead of sharing rewards across participants, every connected miner hashes independently against the network difficulty—if you find a block, you keep the entire reward.

items.title: ShushMiner
items.description: Manufacturer of sound-dampening enclosures for Bitcoin ASIC miners. Targets residential miners who need to reduce noise without custom fabrication.

items.title: Stratum V2
items.description: Stratum V2 (Sv2) is a next-generation mining protocol that addresses the centralization risks inherent in the original Stratum protocol by giving miners control over block template construction.

items.title: Upstream Data
items.description: Bitcoin mining infrastructure company founded by Steve Barbour. Best known for the Ohmm BlackBox: a sound-attenuated, weather-resistant enclosure for ASIC miners.
